title: Webhook key comparison avoids timing and length side channels
description:
src/webhook.rs lines 54-58: the webhook key comparison hashes both the expected and provided keys with SHA-256 before doing constant-time comparison via subtle::ConstantTimeEq. This is better than naive constant-time comparison of raw strings because:

1. SHA-256 normalizes length, preventing length-oracle attacks (comparing strings of different lengths typically short-circuits or leaks length via timing)
2. The ct_eq operates on fixed-size 32-byte digests
3. The subtle crate is the Rust ecosystem standard for constant-time ops

This pattern is worth preserving. A future contributor might be tempted to simplify to == or even a 'fancier' HMAC scheme that introduces timing variability.
